evals.name: ecommerce-requirements-table
evals.prompt: Ich habe hier eine Requirements-Tabelle für unsere neue E-Commerce-Plattform ShopNow. Kannst du daraus eine Context Map nach Domain-Driven Design erstellen, die Bounded Contexts beschreiben und vorschlagen, welche Module in den jeweiligen Contexts Sinn ergeben würden? Die Datei liegt unter eval1_ecommerce_requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: Mermaid context map with contexts like Ordering/Checkout, Fulfillment/Warehouse, Billing/Invoicing, Support, Identity, Pricing (Generic, external SaaS), Shipping Carrier Integration (Conformist/ACL due to legacy SOAP API). Bounded context table, relationship table with justified patterns, module proposals per context, gap analysis.
assertions: Output contains a fenced mermaid code block representing the context map (flowchart with nodes and labeled edges)
At least 5 distinct Bounded Contexts are identified (e.g. Ordering/Checkout, Fulfillment/Warehouse, Billing/Invoicing, Support, Identity, Pricing)
Pricing context is classified as Generic Subdomain, citing REQ-017/REQ-018 (third-party SaaS, no custom logic)
Shipping carrier integration is modeled with Conformist or Anticorruption Layer pattern, citing REQ-019 (legacy SOAP, uninfluenceable)
Identity/authentication sharing (REQ-016) is reflected as an Open Host Service or Shared Kernel relationship to downstream contexts
A Bounded Contexts table exists with columns for Context, Type, Core Purpose, Ubiquitous Language, and Source REQ-IDs
A Relationships table exists citing specific REQ-IDs as rationale for each strategic pattern chosen
A Proposed Modules per Context section exists with at least one module per identified context, each citing Source REQ-IDs
A Gaps/Open Boundary Questions section exists naming at least one concrete ambiguity with a REQ-ID

evals.name: versicherung-lastenheft-raw
evals.prompt: Anbei ein Auszug aus unserem Lastenheft für die Kfz-Versicherung SicherFahrt (roher Fließtext, keine REQ-IDs). Erstelle bitte eine DDD Context Map mit Bounded-Context-Beschreibungen und Modulvorschlägen je Context. Datei: eval2_versicherung_lastenheft.md.
evals.expected_output: Assigns REQ-IDs first. Identifies contexts like Antragstellung/Underwriting, Vertragsverwaltung, Schadenbearbeitung/Claims, Buchhaltung, external Bonitätsprüfung (Conformist/ACL, external SCHUFA). Flags Bonus-Malus feedback loop between Claims and Underwriting as a relationship (Customer/Supplier or Partnership) with rationale. Module proposals, gap analysis on ambiguous boundaries.
assertions: Sequential REQ-IDs (REQ-001, REQ-002, ...) are assigned to the raw text before modeling, since the source has none
Antragstellung/Underwriting/Risikoprüfung is modeled as a distinct Bounded Context from Vertragsverwaltung
Schadenbearbeitung/Claims is modeled as its own Bounded Context, distinct from Vertragsverwaltung and Antragstellung
The Bonus-Malus feedback loop from Schadenbearbeitung back to Antragstellung/Risikoprüfung is explicitly modeled as a relationship with a named strategic pattern and rationale
The external Bonitätsprüfung/SCHUFA integration is classified as Conformist or Anticorruption Layer, noting the system cannot auto-reject based on score alone
Buchhaltung (accounting/billing) is modeled as a separate Bounded Context from Vertragsverwaltung
Output contains a fenced mermaid code block for the context map
A Gaps/Open Boundary Questions section names at least one concrete boundary ambiguity referencing a REQ-ID

evals.name: logistik-functional-spec-input
evals.prompt: Wir haben bereits eine funktionale Spezifikation (Klassendiagramm + Gherkin Acceptance Criteria) für unsere Logistik-Plattform FreightLink erstellt (siehe eval3_logistik_functional_spec.md). Kannst du darauf aufbauend eine DDD Context Map ableiten, die Bounded Contexts beschreiben und passende Module je Context vorschlagen?
evals.expected_output: Uses existing class diagram entities to cluster contexts: Shipment Management/Dispatch, Route & Fleet Planning, Customs/Compliance (ACL to external customs authority per REQ-110), Warehousing (possibly external system), Billing/Invoicing. Relationships traced to REQ-101..110. Module proposals with aggregates (e.g. Shipment + Consignment aggregate). Gap analysis reuses/extends the open gaps already noted in the input spec.
assertions: Reuses entities from the existing class diagram (Shipment, Consignment, Carrier, Route, Vehicle, Driver, CustomsDeclaration, Invoice, WarehouseSlot) rather than re-deriving a domain model from scratch
Customs/Compliance is modeled as its own Bounded Context with an Anticorruption Layer relationship to the external customs authority, citing REQ-110
Shipment/Dispatch and Route & Fleet Planning (or equivalent) are modeled as distinct contexts rather than one monolithic context
At least one aggregate is proposed with an explicit root (e.g. Shipment as aggregate root containing Consignment)
Relationships table cites specific REQ-IDs (REQ-101 through REQ-110) as evidence
Output contains a fenced mermaid code block for the context map
Gap analysis references or extends the ambiguities already noted in the input spec (e.g. ownership of CustomsDeclaration, WarehouseSlot, Invoice)
